findings.description: Loss of complex table header structure and incorrect parsing of spanned cells.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the table header has multiple tiers with cells spanning multiple columns (e.g., dates and report numbers spanning the value and qualifier columns). The HTML flattens this by creating explicit 'Value' and 'Qualifier' columns and concatenating all parent header text into each cell. Furthermore, the text 'no NC standards for dissolved metals', which spans multiple rows in the rightmost columns of the PDF, is split across two rows and duplicated across two columns in the HTML.
